agronomist_remarks: Flooded/puddled soil essential; Zn deficiency most common in India; ICAR CRRI RDF: 80-40-40 kg NPK/ha (Kharif HYV); hybrid rice 100-60-60; split N in 3 doses; Zn @ 25 kg ZnSO4/ha on deficient soils

Timely sowing critical; ICAR-IIWR RDF 120:60:40 kg NPK/ha; max 150-175 kg N for HYV irrigated; split urea (1/3 basal + 1/3 CRI + 1/3 ear emergence); Zn/Fe deficiency on calcareous soils; S @ 20 kg/ha on S-deficient soils
ICAR IIMR RDF: 120:60:40 kg NPK/ha; research shows max N response at 160 kg/ha; side-dress N at V6; Zn @ 10 kg ZnSO4·7H2O/acre basal; Fe deficiency on alkaline soils; luxury K consumption common
Excellent drought tolerance; Al toxicity risk on acidic soils
Most drought tolerant cereal; suited to marginal lands; no waterlogging
Rich in Ca & Fe; tolerates low-fertility soils; intercrop with legumes
Most salt-tolerant cereal; B deficiency can cause sterility

Rhizobium (Mesorhizobium) inoculation mandatory; fixes 40-80 kg N/ha; starter N 15-20 kg/ha; P @ 40-60 kg P2O5/ha basal; ICAR RDF: 20:60:40 kg NPK/ha; Zn & B deficiency on alkali soils; Mo essential for nodulation

Deep-rooted; fixes 40-200 kg N/ha; ICAR IIPR RDF: 20:50:20 kg NPK/ha; P response highest at 50-60 kg P2O5/ha; Phytophthora root rot under waterlogging; medium-duration 170 days, long-duration 240+ days

ICAR RDF: 15-20:40-60:20-30 kg NPK/ha; Rhizobium inoculation adds 20-25 kg N/ha equivalent; sensitive to waterlogging; Mo (1-2 kg/ha as ammonium molybdate) essential for Rhizobium activity; Zn @ 25 kg ZnSO4/ha on deficient soils
ICAR RDF: 15-20:35-40:20-25 kg NPK/ha; Bradyrhizobium inoculation; short duration 55-65 days; excellent Zaid/summer crop; Mo & Zn critical; salt-sensitive; Zn @ 20-25 kg ZnSO4/ha on deficient soils
ICAR Kharif 2025 RDF: 15:35:15 kg NPK/ha; Bradyrhizobium inoculation critical; P dose of 35 kg P2O5/ha is key; higher humidity tolerance than mung; waterlogging risk at pod-fill stage

IISS RDF: 20:60:20 kg NPK/ha (standard); Fe-deficiency chlorosis major issue on pH>7; Bradyrhizobium japonicum seed inoculation mandatory; Zn @ 25 kg ZnSO4/ha; Mo @ 1 kg/ha enhances nodulation; S @ 20-30 kg/ha for protein synthesis

ICAR RDF: 20:60:80 kg NPK/ha; Ca (gypsum 400-500 kg/ha) must reach pegs directly; B @ 1-2 kg/ha & Zn @ 25 kg ZnSO4/ha often deficient; K must be applied as MOP not SOP; avoid waterlogging during pegging

ICAR DRMR RDF: 120:60:40 kg NPK/ha + S 30-40 kg/ha mandatory for oil quality and glucosinolate synthesis; gypsum or SSP preferred S source; B (1-2 kg/ha) prevents hollow stem; Zn @ 25 kg ZnSO4/ha on deficient soils

ICAR RDF: 90:60:60 kg NPK/ha + B 1.5-2 kg/ha (borax) critical for pollen viability and seed set; high K demand; S @ 20-30 kg/ha improves oil content; tolerates slightly acidic soils; Mg deficiency on sandy soils

Very sensitive to waterlogging (dies within 48h); ICAR RDF: 40-50:30-40:20-30 kg NPK/ha; high temperature tolerant; P critical for oil synthesis; no excess N (promotes vegetative growth, reduces oil); S @ 20 kg/ha improves oil quality
Sensitive to drought at flowering; Zn & B often limiting

ICAR CPRI RDF: 150:100:150 kg NPK/ha (irrigated); N max 200-220 kg/ha for very high-yield; K:N ratio 1.5:1 critical for tuber quality; Ca & B prevent hollow heart; Mg @ 40-50 kg MgSO4/ha for chlorophyll; Zn @ 25 kg ZnSO4/ha

TNAU RDF open varieties: 150:100:50 kg NPK/ha; hybrid fertigation: 200:250:250 kg NPK/ha; Ca (calcium nitrate 100-150 kg/ha) prevents blossom-end rot; Mg (MgSO4 foliar) at fruiting stage; Fe/Mn balance critical; B @ 1-2 kg/ha for fruit set

ICAR RDF: 100:50:50 kg NPK/ha + S 20-30 kg/ha (critical for pungency and allicin synthesis); split N in 3 doses (basal 50% + 30 DAT + 45 DAT); Ca for dry matter and shelf life; B (1 kg/ha) for bulb development; avoid excess N after bulb initiation

ICAR RDF: 120:80:60 kg NPK/ha + B 1.5-2 kg/ha (borax) basal; B deficiency causes browning/hollow curd; Mo (0.5 kg/ha sodium molybdate) prevents whiptail; Ca prevents tip-burn; split N in 3 doses; high humidity essential for curd development
Highest Mg demand among solanaceae; Fe & Zn foliar beneficial; K for fruit quality
Requires warm conditions; Mg & Zn key for fruit set; Fe foliar at flowering
Deep, loose soil essential for taproot; B & Ca prevent tip burn; avoid excess N
Fe critical for human nutrition value; avoid pH>7.5 (Fe & Mn lock-up); rapid N uptake

TNAU RDF (tissue culture): 200:100:300 kg NPK/plant/year (Cavendish); highest K demand of any field crop; Mg @ 60-80 kg MgSO4/ha critical (K-Mg antagonism); Zn (25 kg ZnSO4/ha) & B (1-2 kg borax/ha) for bunch quality; drip fertigation recommended; fertilizer application must complete within 100-120 DAP

ICAR NRC Mango RDF: per tree >10 yr 1 kg each NPK (2.2 kg Urea + 1.6 kg SSP + 1.7 kg MOP); Zn deficiency causes "little leaf"; B for fruit set; alternate bearing managed by K application; KNO3 foliar spray for flower induction; Mg foliar at fruit development

ICAR RDF: 600 g N + 200 g P2O5 + 600 g K2O per tree/year (bearing); Fe, Zn & Mn chelate foliar sprays 3-4 times/year; Mg (MgSO4 0.5% foliar) essential; K for juice quality and TSS; B (borax 0.3%) prevents flower drop; Zn 0.5% foliar for fruit size
K:Mg ratio critical; B for berry set; Ca for berry firmness; Zn for shoot elongation
Highly adaptable; Zn & B for fruit quality; avoid waterlogging
Waterlogging kills within 24–48h; B & Ca critical; high Mg for latex production

ICAR SBI RDF (plant): 250:80:120 kg NPK/ha; ratoon: 275-300 kg N; Si application (200 kg silica/ha) improves disease resistance and stalk strength; split N in 3-4 doses; K for juice quality and sucrose accumulation; S @ 30 kg/ha improves CCS%

ICAR CICR RDF Bt hybrid: 120:60:60 kg NPK/ha; min N 80 kg/ha for economic dose; B (1-2 kg/ha borax) deficiency causes boll shedding; Mg (20 kg MgSO4/ha) for chlorophyll; K prevents premature senescence; S @ 25 kg/ha for lint quality; avoid excess N at boll development
Requires flooding tolerance; Fe & Mn available under flooded conditions; high humidity essential

NO Cl-based K fertilizers; K₂SO₄ only (Cl reduces leaf quality); ICAR CTRI RDF: 100:60:100 kg NPK/ha; low N after topping promotes leaf maturity; Mg (MgSO4 foliar) for leaf color; S @ 20 kg/ha for leaf aroma; split K application improves nicotine distribution

ICAR RDF: 150:60:120 kg NPK/ha + FYM 30-40 t/ha; K critical for curcumin content (higher K → higher curcumin); Zn (25 kg ZnSO4/ha) & B (1-2 kg borax/ha) for rhizome quality; Mg @ 30-40 kg MgSO4/ha; partial shade (50%) beneficial in summer; mulching essential

ICAR RDF: 150:60:90 kg NPK/ha + FYM 25-30 t/ha; shade 50% in summer essential; ICAR IISR recommends 112:50:75 kg NPK/ha with drip fertigation; Fe & Mg for yield; Rhizome wilt (Pythium) is major threat under waterlogging; high OM (2.5%+) critical

ICAR RDF: 100:80:60 kg NPK/ha + Ca 50 kg (as CaNO3) prevents blossom-end rot; B 1-2 kg/ha for fruit set; Mg (MgSO4 0.5% foliar) & Fe for capsaicin synthesis; Zn & Fe foliar at flowering; split N in 3 doses (basal 50% + 30 DAT + 60 DAT)
Short duration; B & Zn for seed yield; sensitive to waterlogging
Drought tolerant; salt tolerant; high B demand for oil quality

Fixes 100-200 kg N/ha; ICAR RDF: 20:80:40 kg NPK/ha; Mo (1-2 kg/ha) critical for Rhizobium; highly responsive to P (P:N ratio matters); multi-cut (4-6 cuts/season); salt-tolerant to 8 dS/m; excellent winter fodder; grows best with assured irrigation

Deep taproot (>6m); requires well-limed soil (pH 6.5-7.0); ICAR RDF: 20:80:60 kg NPK/ha; Mo & B for nodulation efficiency; K critical for regrowth after each cut; Ca (lime) for maintaining pH; Mn & Zn deficiency on alkaline soils; 6-8 cuts/year possible

UPASI/TRI India RDF: 150-200 kg N/ha/year (split 6-8 doses); grows ONLY on acid soils (pH 4.5-5.5); Al toxicity > pH 6; Mg (MgSO4 50 kg/ha) & Zn (25 kg ZnSO4/ha) for flush quality; Fe abundant at low pH; Mn availability high at pH 4.5-5.5; K for cold hardiness

CCRI India RDF: 100:50:100 g NPK/plant/year (bearing); shade-grown preferred (30-50%); B (borax 0.3% foliar) & Zn (ZnSO4 0.5% foliar) for berry development; Mg (MgSO4 0.5%) for photosynthesis at altitude; Ca critical for bean quality; K for cup quality

CPCRI India RDF: 500g N + 320g P2O5 + 1200g K2O per palm/year (adult bearing); highest K demand in plantation crops; Cl-tolerant (use KCl); B (borax 50g/palm) for button shedding prevention; Mn critical for West Coast tall; Fe & Zn deficiency on reclaimed soils; Mg @ 500g MgSO4/palm

RRII India RDF immature (yr 1-4): 130-175:35:80 kg NPK/ha; mature: 100:25:75 kg NPK/ha; Mg (MgSO4 50 kg/ha) critical for latex production; Fe & Mn available at low pH; no calcareous soils (stunted growth, Fe chlorosis above pH 6); cover crops essential for soil N; Zn for shoot elongation
